ocr.text: The data presented in the table offers a comprehensive comparison of various plasmid constructs and their corresponding
parameters in relation to HER2 fluorescence in situ hybridization (FISH) assays. Specifically, the metrics for the mean values
and standard deviations (Mean ± SD) were calculated for the plasmids pTRC99Z and two experimental variants, WD3 and
JPJ05C.
Yield (mol/mol)*  SUC (g/L)  PYR (g/L)  LAC (g/L)  FOR (g/L)  ACE (g/L)
Plasmids  WD3  JPJ05C  WD3  JPJ05C  WD3  JPJ05C  WD3  JPJ05C  WD3  JPJ05C  WD3  JPJ05C
Manual HER2 FISH  Automated HER2 FISH  Correlation analysis  Linear regression analysis
Parameter  Mean ± SD  Mean ± SD  Pearson correlation coefficient  P value  Slope  Intercept  R square
Proportion of carcinoma cells with HER2/CEP17 ratio >2.2 (%)  46.6 ± 41.6  41.1 ± 39.4  0.970  0.01  0.919  −1.732  0.942
Goal or response target (mm Hg)  T40/A10 versus A10, OR (95% CI)  T80/A10 versus A10, OR (95% CI)
These metrics are crucial for understanding the performance and reliability of the assays, as they indicate the variability and
central tendencies in the fluorescence measurements obtained from both Manual HER2 FISH and Automated HER2 FISH
techniques. The distinct values reported for WD3 and JPJ05C, particularly at concentrations of 0.27, 0.61, and 4.02, suggest
differences in their effectiveness in amplifying HER2 signals, which may have significant implications for diagnostic
applications. As such, this analysis not only highlights the performance of these plasmid constructs but also underscores the
potential for optimization in HER2 assessment methodologies, paving the way for improved clinical outcomes in oncology.
